description: DB-backed integration: creating a draft definition, publishing version 1, and inserting a submission succeeds with the new core tables and tenant scoping
DB-backed integration: publishing a new version snapshots form and provider config immutably so version 1 remains unchanged after draft edits and republish
DB-backed integration: archiving a definition preserves versions and historical submissions while removing the definition from new portal discovery
Registry boot: CE-only startup registers exactly the built-in ticket, basic-form, and basic-visibility providers and still loads the feature cleanly
Registry boot: EE startup adds workflow, advanced form, and advanced visibility providers through the enterprise entrypoint without changing CE persistence shape
Admin UI: list page supports create blank, create from template, duplicate, archive, and unarchive flows
Admin UI: editor exposes basics, linkage, form, execution, and publish sections with current draft versus published state
Admin workflow: saving a partial draft succeeds but publishing blocks when required configuration is missing
Templates: instantiating a starter template produces a detached editable draft rather than a live reference to the template source
Linked service picker: admins can search the existing service catalog and select a linked service from the editor
Publish validation: definitions with a missing linked service are rejected while definitions with no linked service at all can still publish
Basic builder: short text, long text, checkbox, and date fields can be authored and serialized into the neutral draft schema
Basic builder: select fields preserve option lists and static defaults through preview and publish
Basic builder: add, remove, and reorder operations keep stable field keys when presentation-only properties change
Publish validation: duplicate or invalid field keys are rejected before a definition can be published
Portal layout: a first-class Request Services navigation item appears in the authenticated client portal
Portal catalog: only published definitions that pass visibility evaluation appear in the Request Services landing page and category grouping is applied when present
Portal detail: opening a service request renders the published version snapshot rather than the current mutable draft
Portal detail: static default values are applied on first render for supported fields
Portal submit: a request containing a required file upload persists both the submission and attachment references on success
Portal submit: required-field validation blocks submission against the published snapshot
Authorization: direct submit to a hidden or unauthorized definition is rejected even if the user bypasses catalog listing
Submission flow: the system writes a durable submission before provider execution and returns a stable request id on success
Portal history: My Requests lists only the authenticated user's client-owned submissions and request detail renders their submitted answers
Portal history: attachment references appear in request detail when the submission included uploaded files
Ticket-only provider: submitting a request creates a ticket using configured defaults and mapped request data
Ticket-only provider failure: ticket creation errors leave the submission persisted with a failure status and error summary
Ticket-backed UX: confirmation and history surfaces show the linked ticket reference after successful ticket creation
Catalog-link flexibility: a definition with no linked service still publishes, renders, and submits successfully through the ticket-only path
Admin submissions: MSP can list submissions for a definition and open a detail view that includes payload plus downstream references
Workflow provider registry: EE startup makes workflow-only and ticket-plus-workflow execution modes available in the editor
Workflow-only provider: submitting a request starts the configured workflow and stores its execution reference on the submission
Ticket-plus-workflow provider: the workflow input mapping receives both submission data and the created ticket id
Workflow provider failure: workflow startup errors keep the submission persisted and mark execution failure
Workflow-backed UX: request-history views expose workflow references when the selected provider creates them
Advanced form behavior: conditional show/hide rules affect rendered fields in the portal request form
Advanced form behavior: required validation respects conditional visibility so hidden required fields do not block submit
Advanced defaults: requester/client/context-aware default values resolve into the portal form before submission
Advanced visibility: unauthorized client users do not see restricted definitions and cannot submit them directly
Enterprise authoring: provider-specific EE config panels only render when the corresponding providers are registered
CE authoring: workflow and advanced-form options are absent when EE registrations are not present
Client ownership: list, detail, submit, and attachment access all enforce the same authenticated client scoping rules
Attachment authorization: a portal user cannot fetch or view submission attachments that belong to another client context
History integrity: renaming linked services or categories after submission does not corrupt historical submission rendering
Version integrity: after publishing version 2, a version 1 submission still renders according to version 1 field presentation and values
Republish flow: admins can create a draft from a published definition, change it, and publish version 2 without deleting version 1
Compatibility sanity: existing client portal ticket creation remains functional after the service-request feature is introduced
Compatibility sanity: existing appointment-request flows remain functional after the service-request feature is introduced
